October 2022 • St. Pete Catalyst

Vitale Bros. and SHINE:
This time, it’s personal

For over 30 years, the Vitale Bros. moniker has been synonymous with creative painting, stylized murals, and commercial work across St. Petersburg. However, our 2022 contribution to the SHINE Mural Festival's "Bright Spots" program held an incredibly special place in our hearts.

We had the profound honor of painting a vibrant new mural for the Parc Center for Disabilities. The center was deeply connected to our family history—our late Aunt Nancy, who was diagnosed with Down syndrome, lived in a group home nearby and discovered her own hidden talents as an artist while attending Parc during the last decade of her life.

Working alongside Parc residents and staff to incorporate their new logo into the design wasn’t just a public art installation; it was a deeply personal tribute to family and community. "This is going to cheer them up, and it cheers me up to know that," Johnny Vitale shared during the project. "This is not just for the general public – it’s for them."

Want the full story?

Read the complete feature by Bill DeYoung on the St. Pete Catalyst website.
